If Agastya is rough, he causes diseases; if russet, drought; if
smoky, harm to cattle; if throbbing, fear or danger; if red like
madder, famine and wars; and if tiny, siege of the city,
[Finally the author gives the effects of Agastya's appearance,
colour etc. The metre is TOcrfesren.]

If Canopus shines like gold or crystal and appears to refresh,
as it were, the earth with the tips (streams) of his rays, the earth
will abound in food, making the people happy and prosperous.
[ftrcnlq: is another reading. Utpala interprets the word
as silver For he says :—

160 Brhat Samhita
If Agastya be struck by a meteor or comet, there would cer-
tainly be danger of famine and pestilence. It is said that Canopus
rises when the Sun is stationed In the asterism Hasta and sets when
the Sun goes to Rohini.
[This verse the author employs only to give expression to the
traditional view with regard to Agastya's rising and setting. This
